problem_3.natural_language: The union of the kernels `⋃ n, ker(f^n)` (implemented as `⨆ n, ker(f^n)`) is invariant under `f`.
problem_3.lean:
theorem iSup_ker_pow_mem_invtSubmodule
    {K V : Type*} [CommSemiring K] [AddCommMonoid V] [Module K V]
    (f : Module.End K V) :
    (⨆ n : ℕ, LinearMap.ker (f ^ n)) ∈ f.invtSubmodule
problem_3.natural_language_proof:
Proof: write `W := ⨆ n, ker(f ^ n)`. Take `x ∈ W`. By definition of the supremum, `x` already lies
in `ker(f ^ n)` for some `n` (here we use that `ker(f ^ n)` is an increasing family, so the `⨆` is
just their union). Then `(f ^ n) x = 0`, and applying `f` gives
`(f ^ n) (f x) = (f ^ (n + 1)) x = f ((f ^ n) x) = 0`. Hence `f x ∈ ker(f ^ (n + 1)) ⊆ W`.
So `f` maps `W` into itself, i.e. `W` is invariant.
